steps.title: Identify the conjunctive adverb joining two independent clauses
steps.content: The word however connects two independent clauses: “He did not play drums on the label's classic song 'Rapper's Delight'” and “he joined the label after the song's release.” This signals that a specific punctuation rule must apply.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for conjunctive adverbs
steps.content: When a conjunctive adverb connects two independent clauses, use either IC; however, IC (semicolon before, comma after) or IC. However, IC (period before, comma after). Choice C applies the second pattern correctly—a period after the quotation mark, “However” capitalized to start a new sentence, and a comma after it. Choices A, B, and D each violate this rule through incorrect punctuation patterns. That matches choice C — C is correct.
